metadata.archetype_justification: Gulp is a pure utility library (~55 lines of code) that composes streaming file operations with no persistent state, no database connections, no HTTP server, and no user-specific data.

findings.title: Documented API Interface — Programmatic JavaScript API
findings.description: Gulp exposes a programmatic JavaScript API via exported functions (src, dest, watch, task, series, parallel, registry, tree, lastRun, symlink). Comprehensive documentation exists in docs/api/. No HTTP/REST/GraphQL interface.
findings.gap: No network API surface exists (expected for a library).
findings.recommendation: No action required. The documented programmatic API is the appropriate integration surface.

findings.title: Machine-Readable API Specification — Not Applicable (No HTTP Surface)
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — machine-readable spec is not applicable. Library API contracts are expressed via package.json exports field and typed function signatures.
findings.gap: No TypeScript declarations (.d.ts) for machine-readable type information. Not a deployment gate.
findings.recommendation: Consider adding TypeScript declarations to improve machine-readability for agent tool generation.

findings.title: Structured Error Responses — Not Applicable (No HTTP Surface)
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — structured error responses are not applicable. The library throws JavaScript Error objects with descriptive messages.

findings.title: Idempotent Write Operations — Read-Only Scope
findings.description: Agent scope is read-only. The library has no HTTP write endpoints. File operations (gulp.dest) are filesystem writes controlled by the consuming build script.
findings.gap: Not applicable for read-only scope on a library with no network API.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND library has no HTTP write endpoints.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations. Additionally, gulp is a build tool with no network API — idempotency of file writes is a build-script author concern, not an agent safety concern.

findings.title: Structured Response Format — Node.js Streams
findings.description: Gulp returns Node.js streams (vinyl file objects). CLI output is text-based. Both are appropriate for a build tool library.
findings.gap: Not applicable — no network response format.

findings.title: Rate Limit Documentation — Not Applicable (No HTTP Surface)
findings.description: No HTTP/RPC surface — rate limiting is not applicable. The library is invoked in-process or via CLI.

findings.title: Machine Identity — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: The library does not expose an agent-callable network surface. Authentication is handled by the execution environment (OS user, CI runner identity).
findings.gap: No machine identity mechanism within the library (expected for a build-tool library).
findings.recommendation: No action required at the library level. Ensure CI/CD platforms invoking gulp have proper machine identity controls.

findings.title: Scoped Permissions — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: The library has no authorization model. CI workflow specifies appropriately scoped permissions (contents: write, pull-requests: write).
findings.gap: No library-level permission scoping (expected — consumer responsibility).

findings.title: Action-Level Authorization — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: All exported functions are available to any caller with no authorization layer. Standard for a utility library.
findings.gap: No action-level authorization (expected for a library).

findings.title: Identity Propagation — Not Applicable (Local Execution)
findings.description: No identity propagation mechanism exists. The library makes no service-to-service calls — it operates on the local filesystem.
findings.gap: Not applicable for a local-execution library.

findings.title: Credential Management — Properly Externalized
findings.description: No credentials are managed within the library. CI/CD secrets are externalized via GitHub Actions secrets. No hardcoded credentials found.

findings.title: Audit Logging — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: System does not execute agent-invoked write operations — audit logging is a consumer responsibility. Build executions are logged by CI/CD platforms.
findings.gap: Not applicable for a library with no agent-invocable surface.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND has_auth_surface=false AND has_write_operations=false.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: System does not execute agent-invoked write operations — audit logging is a consumer responsibility. The library/utility is called by applications that own the audit context.

findings.title: Agent Identity Suspension — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: System does not issue or enforce agent identities — suspension is a consumer responsibility.

findings.title: Compensation/Rollback — Not Applicable (No Write Operations)
findings.description: System exposes no write operations — compensation logic is not applicable. Build failures result in incomplete output files which are discarded.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND has_write_operations=false AND has_http_rpc_surface=false.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: The library has no multi-step write workflows. Build failures produce incomplete output which is simply discarded — no persistent state corruption is possible.

findings.title: Concurrency Controls — Read-Only Scope
findings.description: Read-only agent scope. The library provides series() and parallel() for build-time task orchestration, not write-operation concurrency guards.

findings.title: Rate Limiting — Not Applicable (No HTTP Surface)
findings.description: System exposes no HTTP/RPC surface — API-layer rate limiting is not applicable.

findings.title: Blast Radius/Transaction Limits — Read-Only Scope
findings.description: Read-only agents cannot modify records. The library has no transaction concept.

findings.title: Draft/Pending State — Read-Only Scope
findings.description: Read-only agents do not make state changes. The library has no draft/pending state concept.
findings.gap: Not applicable for read-only scope on a build tool.

findings.title: Configurable Approval Gates — Read-Only Scope
findings.description: Read-only agents do not execute write operations. The library has no approval workflow mechanisms.

findings.title: Sandbox/Staging — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: Library/utility — staging environments are a consumer concern. The library has comprehensive CI testing across multiple platforms and Node.js versions.

findings.title: Sensitive Data Classification — Not a Data-Handling Target
findings.description: Gulp is a build tool that reads source files and writes transformed output. It never handles user data, credentials, or regulated information. Stage A = No.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: Stage A = No — not a data-handling target.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Gulp is a build tool that processes source code files (JavaScript, CSS, HTML). It does not store, process, or transmit PII, PHI, financial records, or credentials. The data it handles is developer-authored source code, not user data.

b1.reasoning: Stage A = No — B1 not evaluated.

b2.reasoning: Stage A = No — B2 not evaluated.

b3.reasoning: Stage A = No — B3 not evaluated.

findings.title: Data Residency — Not Applicable (No Data Store)
findings.description: No persistent data store and no user-data logging — residency requirements do not apply. Gulp processes local filesystem content.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: has_persistent_data_store=false AND has_logging_of_user_data=false.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: The library holds no persistent data and does not log user data. It processes local filesystem content during builds with no cross-region data transmission.

findings.title: Selective Query Support — Glob-Based File Selection
findings.description: The library supports glob-based file selection (gulp.src('src/**/*.js')) for scoping file operations. This is the build-tool equivalent of filtered queries.
findings.gap: Not applicable — no API query interface.

findings.title: System of Record — Not Applicable (Build Tool)
findings.description: The library is not a system of record. It processes source files — the source code repository is the system of record.
findings.gap: Not applicable for a build tool.

findings.title: Temporal Metadata — lastRun() for Incremental Builds
findings.description: Gulp provides lastRun() for incremental build timestamps. File modification times are preserved by vinyl-fs. These are build-time temporal concerns.
findings.gap: Not applicable — no data freshness signaling needed for build tools.

findings.title: PII Redaction — Not Applicable (No User Data)
findings.description: System does not log user data and holds no user data — PII-in-logs risk is not applicable.

findings.title: Data Quality Awareness — Not Applicable (Build Tool)
findings.description: Not applicable for a build tool library. Data quality metrics are relevant for data-handling systems.

findings.title: Schema Versioning — Semver with Release Automation
findings.description: The library uses semantic versioning (v5.0.1) with automated release management (release-please). CHANGELOG.md documents changes. No automated breaking-change detection in CI.
findings.gap: No automated breaking-change detection (no consumer-driven contract tests, no type-checking CI step).
findings.recommendation: Consider adding TypeScript declarations and type-checking to CI to detect breaking changes automatically.

findings.title: Semantically Meaningful Field Names — Excellent
findings.description: All function names are clear and semantically meaningful: src, dest, watch, series, parallel, task, registry, tree, lastRun, symlink. No abbreviations or opaque codes.

findings.title: Data Catalog — Comprehensive API Documentation
findings.description: Extensive API documentation in docs/api/ describing each function's parameters, return types, and usage patterns. Serves as effective metadata layer for agent tool definition.
findings.gap: No formal machine-readable API catalog (TypeScript declarations).
findings.recommendation: TypeScript declarations would provide a machine-readable metadata layer.

findings.title: Distributed Tracing — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: Library/utility — tracing and correlation are consumer concerns. Gulp delegates logging to gulp-cli. Build execution tracing is provided by CI/CD platforms.

findings.title: Alerting — Consumer Responsibility
findings.description: Library/utility — alerting is a consumer concern. CI provides build failure notifications. Coveralls tracks coverage trends.

findings.title: Business Outcome Metrics — Not Applicable (Library)
findings.description: Not applicable for an open-source build tool library. npm download counts and GitHub stars are the relevant metrics.

evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered. Archetype: stateless-utility, agent_scope: read-only. Trigger: Service has operations >30s OR long-running workflows.
Extended question not triggered. Archetype: stateless-utility, agent_scope: read-only. Trigger: Service has state changes (stateful-crud, orchestrator).
Extended question not triggered. Archetype: stateless-utility, agent_scope: read-only. Trigger: Service has persistent state (stateful-crud, data-gateway, orchestrator).
Extended question not triggered. Archetype: stateless-utility, agent_scope: read-only. Trigger: Service has external dependencies (calls other services or external APIs).
Extended question not triggered. Archetype: stateless-utility, agent_scope: read-only. Trigger: Service is P0 priority OR is on the critical path.
